title: Raw SQLite error strings returned to clients in 500 responses
evidence: src/handlers.rs:31-33,46-48,60-62,86-88,107-109 map DB errors to Json(json!({"error": e.to_string()})) with INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
suggestion: Return a generic error message and log the detail server-side to avoid leaking internal DB details

title: journal_mode=MEMORY weakens durability of the persistent books.db
evidence: src/db.rs:5 sets PRAGMA journal_mode=MEMORY; src/main.rs:7 opens a persistent file books.db
suggestion: For the persistent binary path use WAL or the default DELETE journal so a crash mid-transaction cannot corrupt the on-disk DB; MEMORY is fine only for the in-memory test DB

title: All requests serialize through a single Arc<Mutex<Connection>>
evidence: src/lib.rs:10 SharedConn = Arc<Mutex<Connection>>; every handler in src/handlers.rs calls conn.lock().unwrap()
suggestion: A connection pool (e.g. r2d2/deadpool) would allow concurrent reads; acceptable for the task scope but limits throughput

title: TDD process followed per agent log but not verifiable from final artifact
evidence: _agent_stdout.log describes red/green/refactor per endpoint; final tree has tests/ that fully exercise all 6 endpoints (13 tests), consistent with test-first but process itself is not reconstructible from the archive
suggestion: None — noted for prompt-factor (tdd) conformance tracking
